info.title: 1Factory API
info.description:
This API allows you to create and query a number of objects in your 1Factory account.

 The API accepts and returns request and response bodies as JSON, using UTF-8 encoding.

Clients may request that response bodies are compressed using gzip compression by passing 'gzip' in the 'Accept-Encoding' header.

In order to use the API, you need to generate an API key for your organization in the API section of the Settings page.

Each API request must include the API key and your organization's ID as header parameters. You can also find your organization ID in the Settings page.

All date time values used in the API are in the [RFC 3339](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt) date-time format, and include a timezone offset value. Incoming values can specify any timezone offset.

Errors returned by the API include an error code. An explanation of these error codes is available [here](./errors.html).

Usage of the API is rate-limited - current default limits are:
- 60 requests per minute
- 1000 requests per day

Exceeding these limits will result in subsequent API requests returning a 429 error code. The daily request limit resets at midnight UTC.

 API responses include the following headers to allow clients to determine how close they are to exceeding them limits:
- **x-ratelimit-minute-limit** permitted number of API calls per minute
- **x-ratelimit-minute-reset** seconds remaining until minute limit resets
- **x-ratelimit-minute-remaining** number of calls remaining until reaching limit
- **x-ratelimit-day-limit** permitted number of API calls per minute
- **x-ratelimit-day-reset** seconds remaining until minute limit resets
- **x-ratelimit-day-remaining** number of calls remaining until reaching limit

If you find you require a higher usage limit, please contact 1Factory support to discuss your use case.

OrgId.name: x-1factory-org
OrgId.description: ID of your organization in 1Factory. Available in the Settings page of the 1Factory application.

ApiKey.name: x-1factory-key
ApiKey.description: API key for your organization. Generated in the Settings page of the 1Factory application.

page.name: page
page.description: Starting page for queried rows - defaults to 0 (the start of the list). Returned rows will start at index (page * page_size).

pageSize.name: page_size
pageSize.description: The maximum number of records to return from the query. Defaults to 100, maximum value 500.

part_number.name: part_number
part_number.description: Part name / number to filter on.

rev.name: rev
rev.description: Part rev to filter on. Typically used in conjunction with the part_number query parameter.

version_status.name: version_status
version_status.description: Version status to filter on. Approval status is only available to Organizations with plan approvals enabled. Active is the released and highest version.

approval_status.name: approval_status
approval_status.description:
The status of an object that requires approval before releasing or closing.

(Availability depends on Organization settings.)

is_tabulated.name: is_tablulated
is_tabulated.description: If tabulated plans should be filtered on.

is_spec_lib.name: is_spec_lib
is_spec_lib.description: If spec library plans should be filtered on.

customer.name: customer
customer.description: Name of customer to filter on.

supplier.name: supplier
supplier.description: Name of supplier to filter on.

project_identifier.name: project_identifier
project_identifier.description:
Name of project_identifier to filter on. Must come from your organization's Part Master Project Identifier LOV. 

(The name used in the UI for this LOV is configured in your organization's Part Master settings and might not be 'Project Identifier').

operation.name: operation
operation.description:
Operation of the inspection, FAI or Plan. 

(Only available if Inspection By Operation or Plan By Operation are enabled.)

site.name: site
site.description: Location where inspection was performed. Must come from your organization's Sites List of Values.

partNoRev.name: part_no_rev
partNoRev.description: Combination of part number and revision to filter on. Part number and revision should correspond to a Part Master entry. Revision should follow part number, and be separated with a space character.

inspectionType.name: type
inspectionType.description: Type of inspection to filter on, from your organization's Inspection Types List of Values.

faiType.name: type
faiType.description: Type of first-article inspection to filter on.

inspIdent1.name: insp_ident_1
inspIdent1.description: Primary identifier to filter on for an inspection or FAI, for example a Work Order or Purchase Order. For FAIs, this is the 'FAI Number'.

inspIdent2.name: insp_ident_2
inspIdent2.description: Secondary identifier to filter on for an inspection or FAI, for example a Customer Purchase Order or Lot #. For FAIs this is the 'PO Number'.

createdAfter.name: created_after
createdAfter.description: Return objects created after the specified date-time

createdBefore.name: created_before
createdBefore.description: Return objects created before the specified date-time

updatedAfter.name: updated_after
updatedAfter.description: Return objects updated after the specified date-time

updatedBefore.name: updated_before
updatedBefore.description: Return objects updated before the specified date-time

closedAfter.name: closed_after
closedAfter.description: Return only approved or rejected objects closed after the specified date-time.

closedBefore.name: closed_before
closedBefore.description: Return only approved or rejected objects closed before the specified date-time.

401.description: Unauthorized. Missing or invalid API key.

example.message: Invalid api key. Ensure the value provided in the x-1factory-key request header matches the API key in your 1Factory settings page.

InvalidQueryParam.description: Invalid request. A problem with the request query parameters prevents it from being processed.

example.message: Invalid query parameter: '-1' is not a valid value for 'page_size'.

InvalidPathParam.description: Invalid request. A problem with the request path parameters prevents it from being processed.

example.message: Invalid path parameter: 'badID' is not a valid value for 'ID'.

DuplicateInspection.description: Conflict. An inspection using the same identifier(s) and type already exists.

example.message: Duplicate resource: An inspection with insp_ident_1: 'PO-12345', insp_ident_2: 'C-B12345', and inspection_type: 'Final' already exists.

code.description: Error code identifying type of error.

alt_part_number.description: An alternative identifier used to identify a part, such as Drawing #, or customer / supplier part number.

alt_rev.description: An alternate part revision, such as a drawing revision, or customer / supplier part revision.

alt_part_number2.description: A second alternative identifier used to identify a part, such as Drawing #, or customer / supplier part number.

alt_rev2.description: A second alternate part revision, such as a drawing revision, or customer / supplier part revision.

unit.description: Unit used for determine part cost. (Optional)

is_assembly.description: If true, indicates part is an assembly. (Optional)

is_itar.description: If true, indicates part is an ITAR part with additional controls. (Optional)

is_buy.description: If true, indicates part is bought, rather than made. (Optional)

comments.example: This is a brand new part that we are prototyping.

alt_part_number.description: An alternative identifier used to identify a part, such as Drawing #, or customer / supplier part number.

alt_rev.description: An alternate part revision, such as a drawing revision, or customer / supplier part revision.

alt_part_number2.description: A second alternative identifier used to identify a part, such as Drawing #, or customer / supplier part number.

alt_rev2.description: A second alternate part revision, such as a drawing revision, or customer / supplier part revision.

plan_count.description: The number of plans this Part Master is associated with.

ncr_count.description: The number of NCRs this Part Master is associated with.

type.description: Identify if the part is part of a regular part, tabulated part, or the parent tabulated part

parent_part_number.description: The part number of the parent part if this part is a Table of Size entry.

parent_rev.description: The part revision of the parent part if this part is a Table of Size entry.

unit.description: Unit used for determine part cost. (Optional)

is_library.description: If true, indicates the part is setup as a library for a Spec Library.

is_assembly.description: If true, indicates part is an assembly.

is_itar.description: If true, indicates part is an ITAR part with additional controls.

has_ppap.description: If true, indicates part has PPAP documents.

is_buy.description: If true, indicates part is bought, rather than made. (Optional)

comments.example: This is a brand new part that we are prototyping.

lot_size.description: The lot size for this order. (Optional).

approval_status.description:
The status of a plan that requires approval before releasing or closing.

(Availability depends on Organization settings.)

is_tabulated.description: If the plan is for a tabulated part.

is_spec_lib.description: If the plan is a spec library

inspection_method.description: How the feature should be inspected.

sampling_rule.description: How frequently should the feature be checked.

data_type.description: How the data for this specification is recorded - numerically, as a pass/fail, or calculated from other features.

approval_status.description:
The status of a plan that requires approval before releasing or closing.

(Availability depends on Organization settings.)

is_tabulated.description: If the plan is for a tabulated part.

is_spec_lib.description: If the plan is a spec library

site.description: Location where inspection was performed, from your organization's Site LOV.

in_spec_pct.description: Percentage of in-spec parts for this inspection.

inspected_by_name.description: Name of user that last set inspection status.

inspected_on.description: Date & time that inspection status set.

reviewed_by_name.description: Name of user that set review status.

reviewed_on.description: Date & time that review status was set.

ncrs.description: List of NCR #s associated with this inspection

site.description: Location where inspection was performed, from your organization's Site LOV.

in_spec_pct.description: Percentage of in-spec parts for this inspection.

inspected_by_name.description: Name of user that last set inspection status.

inspected_on.description: Date & time that inspection status set.

reviewed_by_name.description: Name of user that set review status.

reviewed_on.description: Date & time that review status was set.

ncrs.description: List of NCR #s associated with this inspection

InspectionDetail.description: Detailed inspection data, including feature specifications and measurement data.

site.description: Location where inspection was performed, from your organization's Site LOV.

in_spec_pct.description: Percentage of in-spec parts for this inspection.

inspected_by_name.description: Name of user that last set inspection status.

inspected_on.description: Date & time that inspection status set.

reviewed_by_name.description: Name of user that set review status.

reviewed_on.description: Date & time that review status was set.

ncrs.description: List of NCR #s associated with this inspection

FaiDetail.description: Detailed first-article inspection data, including feature specifications and measurement data.

number.description: The reference number for the NCR, Complaint, or CAPA

customer_ident.description: Your Organization's custom identifier for customer NCRs (ex. Customer PO #)

supplier_ident.description: Your Organization's custom identifier for supplier NCRs (ex. Supplier Lot #)

mfg_insp_ident_1.description: Your Organization's custom primary identifier for manufacturing inspection identifiers. (ex. Job #)

mfg_insp_ident_2.description: Your Organization's custom secondary identifier for manufacturing inspection identifiers. (ex. PO #)

rec_insp_ident_1.description: Your Organization's custom primary identifier for receiving inspection identifiers. (ex. Job #)

rec_insp_ident_2.description: Your Organization's custom secondary identifier for receiving inspection identifiers. (ex. PO #)

lot_quantity.description: The number of parts in the lot of the NCR

inspection_quantity.description: The number of parts in the lot that were inspected as part of the NCR

defective_quantity.description: The number of parts rejected as part of the NCR

detected_at.description: Where the problem for the NCR was detected.

problem_type.description: A simple description of the problem.

root_cause.description: The root cause of the problem.

caused_by.description: The department or group that caused the problem.

task_status.description: Status of all tasks on NCR.

task_count.description: Number of tasks for the NCR.

use_as_is_quantity.description: The number of parts that failed inspection that can be used as-is without rework or scrapping.

scrap_quantity.description: The number of parts that failed inspection that must be scrapped.

return_quantity.description: The number of parts that failed inspection that must be returned to the supplier.

rework_quantity.description: The number of parts that failed inspection that can be reworked to pass inspection.

repair_quantity.description: The number of parts that failed inspection that can be repaired to pass inspection.

no_defect_quantity.description: The number of parts in the lot that do not have any defects.

rma_number.description: RMA number, if any, for the returned parts in the NCR.

shipment.description: Shipment number, if any, for parts in the NCR

mrb_meeting_notes.description: Any meeting notes for this NCR from a MBR meeting.

date_due.description: Date & time that a CAPA is due.
Date & time that a Complaint is due.

complaint_on.description: Date & time of the original complaint.

Specification.description: Specification that defines feature to be measured.

sheet_zone.description: Sheet and zone of the specification

place.description: Place number - for features that have multiple places, feature specification is repeated for each place.

characteristic.description: Characteristic being measured, or, in the case of a note, the text of the note.

dimension_type.description: Dimension type for this specification - Standard, Manufacturing or Deviation.

referenced_feature.description: Balloon number of specification that this feature references, for example a manufacturing dimension referencing the actual drawing dimension it corresponds to.

data_type.description: How the data for this specification is recorded - numerically, as a pass/fail, or calculated from other features.

lower_spec_limit.description: Lower specification limit for the feature.

upper_spec_limit.description: Upper specification limit for the feature.

unit.description: Unit of measure for this feature.

descriptor_datum.description: Descriptor of feature. For GD&T, this is used to record the datum.

bonus_tolerance.description: For GD&T features, defines the material condition for evaluating bonus tolerance, if applicable.

label.description: Optional label to add more information to the specification.

inspection_method.description: How the feature should be inspected.

sampling_rule.description: How frequently should the feature be checked.

is_key.description: Indicates if feature is key for quality.

PartData.description: Inspection data for a single part.

grp_ident.description: Identifier for a grouping of parts.

row_ident.description: Identifier for a unique part, sucha as a serial number.

updated_on.description: Date & time that data for this part was last updated.

measurements.description: List of measurements recorded for part. Order of entries corresponds to order of specifications, so index of a measurement corresponds to the index of the feature specification & place it is for.

value.description: Actual measurement recorded. For a pass/fail specification, 1.0 corresponds to PASS, 0.0 corresponds to FAIL.

bonus.description: For GD&T specs that allow a bonus tolerance, the bonus tolerance to apply to actual measurement.

ID.description: Unique identifier for the Inspection, assigned by 1Factory.

insp_ident_1.description: Primary identifier for an inspection or FAI, for example a Work Order or Purchase Order. For FAIs, this is the 'FAI Number'. Must be unique for each entry when used in a Work Order List.

insp_ident_2.description: Secondary identifier for an inspection or FAI, for example a Customer Purchase Order or Lot #. For FAIs this is the 'PO Number'. (Optional)

insp_ident_3.description: Tertiary identifier for an inspection, for example a Customer Purchase Order or Lot #. (Optional)

machines.description: List of machines for the inspection. Only for manufacturing inspections. Entries in this list must match the Mfg. Resources - Machines LOV configured for your organization.

parts_passed.description: The number of parts that pass inspection.

parts_failed.description: The number of parts that failed inspection.

customer_name.description: The name of the customer (for manufacturing inspections).

supplier_name.description: The name of the supplier (for receiving or supplier inspections).

supplier_number.description: The supplier number / vendor code (for receiving or supplier inspections).

direct.description: Indicates if the supplier provides direct materials or services.

indirect.description: Indicates if the supplier provides indirect materials or services.

strategic.description: Indicates if the supplier is considered strategic to the organization.

small_business.description: Indicates if the supplier is classified as a small business.

organization_codes.description: List of organization codes associated with the supplier.

commodity_codes.description: List of commodity codes that the supplier provides.

qualifications.description: List of supplier qualifications and their status history.

certifications.description: List of supplier certifications and their expiration dates.

qualification_status.description: Current qualification status derived from the most recent qualification entry.

last_qualification_date.description: Date and time of the most recent qualification assessment.

re_qualification_date.description: Calculated date when the supplier needs to be re-qualified, based on the last qualification date plus frequency in months.

date.description: Date and time when the qualification was assessed or updated.

status.description: Current qualification status of the supplier.

review_status.description: Review status of the qualification assessment.

frequency.description: Review frequency in months for re-qualification.

expiration_date.description: Date and time when the certification expires.

certification.description: Name or type of the certification.

part_number.description: Part number. Together with rev, uniquely identifies a part.

rev.description: Part revision. Together with part number, uniquely identifies a part.

project_identifier.name: project_identifier
project_identifier.description:
Name of project_identifier to filter on. Must come from your organization's Part Master Project Identifier LOV. 

(The name used in the UI for this LOV is configured in your organization's Part Master settings and might not be 'Project Identifier').

inspection_type.description: Type of inspection, as defined in your organization's Inspection Types List of Values

operation.description: Operation of the inspection. No operation specified or an operation of 'All' indicates that an inspection is for all operations in the inspection Plan. (Only for manufacturing inspections, and if the 'Inspections by Op' configuration is enabled for your organization.)

lot_size.description: Number of parts in lot being inspected

SubPartList.description: List of parts that make up this assembly. Each listed part must already exist as a Part Master entry.

quantity.description: Number of instances of this part in the assembly.

owner.description: Name of user that owns the entity.

created_by_username.description: Name of user that created the entity.

created_on.description: Date & time that an object was created.

updated_on.description: Date & time that an object was last updated.

closed_on.description: Date & time that an object was closed.

tags.name: Part Master
tags.description: Part Master defines the part numbers, revisions and other information for parts referenced in Plans, Inspections, FAIs etc.

tags.name: Manufacturing
tags.description: Data related to parts manufactured by your organization.

tags.name: Receiving
tags.description: Data related to parts received by your organization.

tags.name: Customers
tags.description: Data shared with your organization by your customers.

tags.name: Suppliers
tags.description: Data shared with your organization by your suppliers, or details about your suppliers.

tags.name: Work Orders
tags.description: Work Orders define a list of current work order primary & secondary identifiers that may be used as identifiers on Inspections & FAIs.

put.summary: Creates a new (or updates existing) part master entry for your organization.
put.description:
Create a new part master entry that can be used when referring to parts when creating Plans, Inspections, FAIs etc. 

 The part number and rev must be unique - if a part master with the same part number and revision already exists, the existing Part Master entry will be overwritten.

201.description: Created. Successfully created part master entry is returned.

200.description: OK. Successfully updated part master entry is returned.

400.description: Invalid request. A problem with the request prevented it from being processed.

example.message: Invalid request body: 'READY' is not a valid value for 'status'.

get.summary: Gets a List of part masters for your organization.
get.description:
The list of part master entries that can be filtered by specifying query parameters. 
 Paging parameters control the number of rows of part master objects returned. By default, the first 100 part masters are returned.

parameters.name: parent_part_number
parameters.description: Parent Part number to filter on for Table of Size entries.

parameters.name: parent_rev
parameters.description: Parent Part rev to filter on for Table of Size entries.

parameters.name: is_library
parameters.description: If the part is a configured as a library.

parameters.name: is_assembly
parameters.description: If the part is a configured as an assembly.

parameters.name: is_itar
parameters.description: If the part is a configured as an ITAR part.

parameters.name: has_ppap
parameters.description: If the part has any PPAP documents.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: If the part has any PPAP documents.

200.description: Returns a list of part masters, most recently updated first. Utilize paging query parameters to query older part masters. By default the 100 most recent are returned.

400.description: Invalid request. A problem with the request prevented it from being processed.

example.message: Invalid request body: 'READY' is not a valid value for 'status'.

put.summary: Defines assembly relationships between Part Masters.
put.description: Defines (or replaces) the list of parts that make up an assembly part. All referenced part numbers & revisions must already exist as Part Master entries.

example.comments: This is a brand new part that we are prototyping.

200.description: OK. Set assembly on Part Master entry. Part Master entry for which the assembly was created is returned.

400.description: Invalid request. A problem with the request prevented it from being processed.

example.message: Invalid request body: '-1' is not a valid value for 'quantity'.
Not found: Part Master '12-54322-11 B' does not exist.

put.summary: Updates the list of manufacturing work order numbers for your organization.
put.description:
Defines the current list of manufacturing work orders for your organization. If a work order list already exists, this request will overwrite it.

This list will be used to provide suggested values for manufacturing inspection identifiers in the 1Factory application, for example, when creating a new inspection. 

It will also pre-populate the lot size for inspections created using an inspection identifier in this list, if the corresponding list entry has a lot size. 

Note that the names used to refer to inspection identifiers in the 1Factory application will depend on what is configured for Mfg. Insp. Ident. in your organization's settings page.

The combination of insp_ident1 and insp_ident2 uniquely identifies each work order. An error will be returned if the list contains duplicate entries.

400.description: Invalid request. A problem with the request prevented it from being processed.

example.message: Invalid request body: '-1' is not a valid value for 'lot_size'.

put.summary: Updates the list of receiving work order numbers for your organization.
put.description:
Defines the current list of receiving work orders for your organization. If a work order list already exists, this request will overwrite it.

This list will be used to provide suggested values for manufacturing inspection identifiers in the 1Factory application, for example, when creating a new inspection. 

It will also pre-populate the lot size for inspections created using an inspection identifier in this list, if the corresponding list entry has a lot size. 

Note that the names used to refer to inspection identifiers in the 1Factory application will depend on what is configured for Rec. Insp. Ident. in your organization's settings page.

The combination of insp_ident1 and insp_ident2 uniquely identifies each work order. An error will be returned if the list contains duplicate entries.

400.description: Invalid request. A problem with the request prevented it from being processed.

example.message: Invalid request body: '-1' is not a valid value for 'lot_size'.

get.summary: Gets a list of manufacturing plans for your organization.
get.description:
The list of manufacturing plans can be filtered by specifying query parameters.

Paging parameters control the number of rows Plan objects returned. By default, the first 100 plans are returned.

200.description: Returns a list of plans, most recently updated first. Utilize paging query parameters to query older plans. By default the 100 most recent are returned.

get.summary: Gets a detailed manufacturing plan record.
get.description: Returns the specified plan record, including specifications.

200.description: Returns a detailed plan record, including specification data.

example.message: Not found: Manufacturing Plan '12345' does not exist.

get.summary: Gets a list of manufacturing inspections for your organization.
get.description:
The list of manufacturing inspections can be filtered by specifying query parameters.

Paging parameters control the number of rows Inspection objects returned. By default, the first 100 inspections are returned.

200.description: Returns a list of inspections, most recently updated first. Utilize paging query parameters to query older inspections. By default the 100 most recent are returned.

post.summary: Creates a new manufacturing inspection for your organization.
post.description:
A new manufacturing inspection is created, ready for inspection data to be entered. 

 ## Plan Identification 
The plan used by the inspection is identified by part number and rev. An active version of a Plan for the specified part must exist when creating the inspection. 

For Inspections that are for a Plan associated with a ToS part enter the part number and rev for the ToS entry.

 ## Uniqueness 
The inspection is identified by its inspection identifiers and inspection type. A unique combination of work order identifiers and inspection type must be specified when creating a new inspection. If an inspection already exists with the specified identifiers and type, an error will be returned. 

 ## Additional Details
* Customer Name: The name must match the customer name in the system exactly
* Operation: This field is optional, and only valid if your organization is configured for "Inspections by Operation". If enabled, the value must match an operation on the plan, or be blank or not included in the request to default to create all operation inspections for the plan
* Machines - If included, a value must match a machine in the Mfg Resources -> Machines LOV configured for your organization

201.description: Created. Successfully created inspection entry is returned.

400.description: Invalid request. A problem with the request prevented it from being processed.

example.message: Invalid request body: 'Ad-hoc' is not a valid value for 'inspection_type'.

get.summary: Gets a detailed manufacturing inspection record.
get.description: Returns the specified inspection record, including measurement data.

200.description: Returns a detailed inspection record, including measurement data.

example.message: Not found: Manufacturing Inspection '12345' does not exist.

get.summary: Gets a list of receiving plans for your organization.
get.description:
The list of receiving plans can be filtered by specifying query parameters.

Paging parameters control the number of rows Plan objects returned. By default, the first 100 plans are returned.

200.description: Returns a list of plans, most recently updated first. Utilize paging query parameters to query older plans. By default the 100 most recent are returned.

get.summary: Gets a detailed receiving plan record.
get.description: Returns the specified plan record, including specifications.

200.description: Returns a detailed plan record, including specification data.

example.message: Not found: Receiving Plan '12345' does not exist.

get.summary: Gets a list of receiving inspections for your organization.
get.description:
The list of receiving inspections can be filtered by specifying query parameters.

Paging parameters control the number of rows Inspection objects returned. By default, the first 100 inspections are returned.

200.description: Returns a list of inspections, most recently updated first. Utilize paging query parameters to query older inspections. By default the 100 most recent are returned.

post.summary: Creates a new receiving inspection for your organization.
post.description:
A new receiving inspection is created, ready for inspection data to be entered. 

 ## Plan Identification 
The plan used by the inspection is identified by part number and rev. An active version of a Plan for the specified part must exist when creating the inspection. 

For Inspections that are for a Plan associated with a ToS part enter the part number and rev for the ToS entry.

 ## Uniqueness 
The inspection is identified by its inspection identifiers and inspection type. A unique combination of work order identifiers and inspection type must be specified when creating a new inspection. If an inspection already exists with the specified identifiers and type, an error will be returned.

 ## Additional Details
* Supplier Name: The name must match the supplier name in the system exactly

201.description: Created. Successfully created inspection entry is returned.

400.description: Invalid request. A problem with the request prevented it from being processed.

example.message: Invalid request body: 'Ad-hoc' is not a valid value for 'inspection_type'.

get.summary: Gets a detailed receiving inspection record.
get.description: Returns the specified inspection record, including measurement data.

200.description: Returns a detailed inspection record, including measurement data.

example.message: Not found: Receiving Inspection '12345' does not exist.

get.summary: Gets a list of suppliers in your organization.
get.description: The list of suppliers can be filtered by specifying query parameters. Paging parameters control the number of supplier objects returned. By default, the first 100 suppliers are returned.

200.description: Returns a list of suppliers, utilize paging query parameters to query older suppliers. By default the 100 most recent are returned.

get.summary: Get detailed supplier record
get.description: Returns a specific supplier record, including qualification and certification data.

200.description: Returns a detailed inspection record, including measurement data.

example.message: Not found: Supplier '12345' does not exist.

The list of supplier plans can be filtered by specifying query parameters.

Paging parameters control the number of rows Plan objects returned. By default, the first 100 plans are returned.

200.description: Returns a list of plans, most recently updated first. Utilize paging query parameters to query older plans. By default the 100 most recent are returned.

The list of customer plans can be filtered by specifying query parameters.

Paging parameters control the number of rows Plan objects returned. By default, the first 100 plans are returned.

get.description: Returns the specified plan record, including specifications.

get.summary: Gets a list of inspections shared with your organization by your customers.
get.description:
The list of customer inspections can be filtered by specifying query parameters.

Paging parameters control the number of rows Inspection objects returned. By default, the first 100 inspections are returned.

200.description: Returns a list of inspections, most recently updated first. Utilize paging query parameters to query older inspections. By default the 100 most recent are returned.

get.summary: Gets a detailed customer inspection record.
get.description: Returns the specified inspection record, including measurement data.

200.description: Returns a detailed inspection record, including measurement data.

example.message: Not found: Customer Inspection '12345' does not exist.

get.summary: Gets a list of manufacturing first-article inspections for your organization.
get.description:
The list of manufacturing first-article inspections can be filtered by specifying query parameters.

Paging parameters control the number of rows Inspection objects returned. By default, the first 100 inspections are returned.

200.description: Returns a list of first-article inspections, most recently updated first. Utilize paging query parameters to query older inspections. By default the 100 most recent are returned.

post.summary: Creates a new manufacturing first-article inspection for your organization.
post.description:
A new manufacturing first-article inspection is created, ready for inspection data to be entered. 

The plan used by the inspection is identified by part number and rev. An active version of a Plan for the specified part must exist when creating the inspection. 

The inspection is identified by its inspection identifiers and inspection type. A unique combination of work order identifiers and inspection type must be specified when creating a new inspection. If an inspection already exists with the specified identifiers and type, an error will be returned.

201.description: Created. Successfully created first-article inspection entry is returned.

400.description: Invalid request. A problem with the request prevented it from being processed.

example.message: Invalid request body: 'Ad-hoc' is not a valid value for 'fai_type'.

get.summary: Gets a detailed manufacturing first-article inspection record.
get.description: Returns the specified first-article inspection record, including measurement data.

200.description: Returns a detailed first-article inspection record, including measurement data.

example.message: Not found: Manufacturing FAI '12345' does not exist.

400.description: Invalid request. A problem with the request prevented it from being processed.

example.message: Invalid request body: 'Ad-hoc' is not a valid value for 'inspection_type'.
